title: malva cisplatina
side_effects: Malva cisplatina can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps due to its high concentration of anthraquinones. Long-term use can also lead to kidney damage and electrolyte imbalances, resulting in symptoms like fatigue, muscle weakness, and dizziness. Additionally, excessive consumption of malva cisplatina can cause allergic reactions, such as hives and itching, especially in individuals with sensitive skin. If not prepared correctly, malva cisplatina can also lead to severe gastrointestinal issues, including vomiting and abdominal pain.
